Model functions¶

Everyday use mathematical functions and distributions.

brixs.model_functions.Gauss(x, amp, c, sigma)¶

Gaussian distribution.

\[y(x) = \text{amp } e^{-\frac{(x-c)^2}{2 \sigma^2}}\]

where,

\[\text{Area }= \sqrt{2 \pi} \text{ amp } |\sigma|\]

and,

\[\text{fwhm }= 2 \sqrt{2 \ln(2)} \sigma\]
Parameters

  • x – x array

  • amp – Amplitude

  • c – Center

  • sigma – standard deviation

Returns

\(y(x)\)

brixs.model_functions.Lorentz(x, gamma, c)¶

Cauchy–Lorentz distribution.

\[y(x) = \frac{1}{\pi \gamma} \frac{\gamma^2}{\gamma^2 + (x-c)^2}\]

where,

\[\text{Amplitude }= \frac{1}{\pi \gamma}\]

and,

\[\text{fwhm }= 2 \gamma\]

and,

\[\text{Area }= 1\]
Parameters

  • x – x array

  • gamma – Scale factor

  • c – Center

Returns

\(y(x)\)

brixs.model_functions.areaGauss(x, A, c, sigma)¶

Gaussian distribution.

\[y(x) = \frac{\text{Area}}{\sqrt{2\pi} w} e^{-\frac{(x-c)^2}{2 w^2}}\]
Parameters

  • x – x array

  • A – Area

  • c – Center

  • sigma – standard deviation

Returns

\(y(x)\)

brixs.model_functions.fwhmArctan(x, amp, c, w)¶

Arctangent function.

\[y(x) = \frac{A}{\pi} \left[ \arctan(\frac{1}{w}(x-c)) + \frac{\pi}{2} \right]\]
Parameters

  • x – x array

  • amp – Amplitude

  • c – Center

  • w – FWHM (it will take fwhm units to go from amp/4 to (3amp)/4)

Returns

\(y(x)\)

brixs.model_functions.fwhmAreaGauss(x, A, c, w)¶

Gaussian distribution.

\[y(x) = \frac{2 \sqrt{\ln(2)} A}{w \sqrt{\pi}} e^{-\frac{4 \ln(2) (x-c)^2}{w^2}}\]
Parameters

  • x – x array

  • A – Area

  • c – Center

  • w – FWHM

Returns

\(y(x)\)

brixs.model_functions.fwhmAreaLorentz(x, A, c, w)¶

Cauchy–Lorentz distribution.

\[y(x) = A \frac{1}{\pi w} \frac{w^2}{w^2 + (x-c)^2}\]
Parameters

  • x – x array

  • A – Area

  • c – Center

  • w – FWHM

Returns

\(y(x)\)

brixs.model_functions.fwhmAreaVoigt(x, A, c, w, m)¶

Pseudo-voigt curve.

\[y(x) = A \left[ m \frac{1}{\pi w} \frac{w^2}{w^2 + 4 (x-c)^2} + (1-m) \frac{2 \sqrt{\ln(2)}}{w \sqrt{\pi}} e^{-\frac{4 \ln(2) (x-c)^2}{w^2}} \right]\]

where,

\[\text{amp }= \frac{A}{w} \left[ m\frac{1}{\pi} + (1-m)\frac{2\sqrt{\ln(2)}}{\sqrt{\pi}} \right]\]
Parameters

  • x – x array

  • A – is the Area

  • c – Center

  • w – FWHM

  • m – Factor from 1 to 0 of the lorentzian amount

Returns

\(y(x)\)

brixs.model_functions.fwhmErr(x, amp, c, w)¶

Error function. Integal of gaussian function calculated by scipy.special.erf().

\[y(x) = \frac{2}{\sqrt{\pi}} \int_0^x e^{-t^2} dt\]
Parameters

  • x – x array

  • amp – Amplitude

  • c – Center

  • w – FWHM (it will take roughly fwhm units to go from amp/4 to (3amp)/4)

Returns

\(y(x)\)

brixs.model_functions.fwhmGauss(x, amp, c, w)¶

Gaussian distribution.

\[y(x) = \text{amp } e^{-\frac{4 \ln(2) (x-c)^2}{w^2}}\]

where,

\[\text{Area }= \frac{\sqrt{\pi} \text{ amp } w}{2 \sqrt{\ln(2)}}\]
Parameters

  • x – x array

  • A – Amplitude

  • c – Center

  • w – FWHM

Returns

\(y(x)\)

brixs.model_functions.fwhmLorentz(x, amp, c, w)¶

Cauchy–Lorentz distribution.

\[y(x) = \text{amp } \frac{w^2}{w^2 + (x-c)^2}\]

where,

\[\text{Area }= \text{amp } \pi w\]
Parameters

  • x – x array

  • amp – Amplitude

  • c – Center

  • w – FWHM

Returns

\(y(x)\)

brixs.model_functions.fwhmVoigt(x, amp, c, w, m)¶

Pseudo-voigt curve.

\[y(x) = A \left[ m \frac{w^2}{w^2 + (x-c)^2} + (1-m) e^{-\frac{4 \ln(2) (x-c)^2}{w^2}} \right]\]
Parameters

  • x – x array

  • amp – Amplitude

  • c – Center

  • w – FWHM

  • m – Factor from 1 to 0 of the lorentzian amount

Returns

\(y(x)\)

brixs.model_functions.square(x, amp, c, w)¶

Square step function.

\[\begin{split}y(x) = \begin{cases} 0, & \text{ for $x < c-w/2$}\\ \text{amp}, & \text{ for } c-w/2 < x < c+w/2\\ 0, & \text{ for } x > c+w/2\\ \end{cases}\end{split}\]
Parameters

  • x – x array

  • amp – Amplitude

  • c – Center

  • w – FWHM

Returns

\(y(x)\)

brixs
